content:
# elizaos-plugins/registry Daily Update (Apr 5, 2026)

## OVERVIEW 
The registry saw active expansion today with two new plugin submissions. Development discussions focused on establishing cryptographic identity and capability-based authorization layers for agents, with community members proposing integrations for trust-gated tool execution and behavioral fingerprinting.

## KEY TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENTS
*   **Plugin Ecosystem Expansion**
    *   Two new plugins were submitted for registry inclusion, focusing on external service integration and signal processing. ([#334](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/registry/pull/334), [#333](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/registry/pull/333))

## NEWLY OPENED PULL REQUESTS
*   [#334](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/registry/pull/334): Add @madeonsol/plugin-madeonsol
*   [#333](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/registry/pull/333): Add plugin-signalfuse

## CLOSED ISSUES
*   **Cross-Agent Messaging**
    *   [#6418](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/registry/issues/6418): Closed as "not planned." The maintainer noted that agent-to-agent coordination is being refocused toward foundational cryptographic identity rather than standalone messaging protocols like XMTP.

## NEW ISSUES
*   None

## ACTIVE ISSUES
*   **[#6688](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/registry/issues/6688) Plugin Proposal: AgentID**
    *   Discussion continues regarding cryptographic identity and trust layers. Recent updates clarify that trust levels are dynamic and computed server-side based on behavioral baselines (context continuity, risk scores, and scarring). Contributors are comparing evidence schemas and behavioral fingerprinting metrics to ensure compatibility between different agent identity implementations.
*   **[#6707](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/registry/issues/6707) Plugin proposal: @sint/eliza-plugin**
    *   A concrete implementation sketch was provided for a capability-based authorization layer. The proposed plugin uses a middleware pattern to enforce policy rules on tool calls, requiring valid capability tokens before execution. The discussion is currently evaluating whether this should be implemented as a standalone plugin or integrated into the core framework.
